title: Lophiaris lowii
intro: Lophiaris lowii, also known as the Low's air plant or Air Plant, is a type of epiphytic plant that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Lophiaris lowii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Lophiaris lowii is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Lophiaris and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Lophiaris is a group of terrestrial orchids native to Central and South America. Lophiaris lowii is specifically found in the tropical forests of Central America. This orchid species is characterized by its unique flower and growth habits, which distinguish it from other members of its genus.
